52.reason: The loop should start from n-1, because the valid indices for xp are 0 to n-1. Starting at n accesses out-of-bounds memory, causing undefined behavior.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  The loop starts from n, but valid indices are 0 to n-1. Accessing xp[n] is out of bounds.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Loop starts from n, but array indices go from 0 to n-1, so xp[n] is out of bounds. Should start from n-1.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop should start from n-1, because the valid indices for xp are 0 to n-1. Starting at n accesses out-of-bounds memory, causing undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop should start at i = n-1 because valid indices are 0 to n-1; accessing xp[n] is out of bounds and leads to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Array index out of bounds: xp has valid indices from 0 to n-1. Accessing xp[n] causes undefined behavior/segmentation fault. Should be initialized to n-1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Array index out of bounds: xp has valid indices 0 to n-1, accessing xp[n] causes undefined behavior and likely a segmentation fault.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop starts at i = n, which is out of bounds since valid indices are 0 to n-1. Also, when i=0, i-j may become negative causing invalid memory access.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop starts at i = n, which is out of bounds since xp indices are 0 to n-1. Also, loop condition i >= 0 causes access to xp[n] and potentially xp[-1] after decrementing, leading to undefined behavior.]

56.reason: Error: xp[i] is out of bounds when i = n. Also, the logic for combining like terms is flawed; it modifies xp[i] but then later processes xp[i-j] which may have been modified incorrectly.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  xp[i] is out of bounds when i = n.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: xp[i] is out of bounds when i = n. Also, the logic for combining like terms is flawed; it modifies xp[i] but then later processes xp[i-j] which may have been modified incorrectly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logical error: merging terms by accumulating backwards while modifying the loop variable later is incorrect and leads to skipped terms or incorrect coefficient sums.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When i == n, xp[i] is an uninitialized pointer (out-of-bounds), leading to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Accesses xp[i] when i may be n (invalid index) or negative after loop updates, causing undefined behavior.]

58.reason: Merging logic does not zero out xp[i-j]->xishu, leaving the data structure inconsistent and potentially causing duplicate outputs if the skipping logic fails.

66.reason: Incorrect index adjustment logic. Combined with the for-loop's decrement, it attempts to skip merged terms but relies on fragile arithmetic that can lead to incorrect iteration or missed terms.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrect index adjustment logic. Combined with the for-loop's decrement, it attempts to skip merged terms but relies on fragile arithmetic that can lead to incorrect iteration or missed terms.
